mission.title: containerd: CRI support specify snapshotter during pod creation instead of a global config
mission.description: CRI support specify snapshotter during pod creation instead of a global config. Requested by 13+ users.

steps.title: Check current containerd deployment
steps.description:
Verify your containerd version and configuration:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n containerd -l app.kubernetes.io/name=containerd
helm list -n containerd 2>/dev/null || echo "Not installed via Helm"
```
This feature requires a working containerd installation.

steps.title: Review containerd configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant containerd configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n containerd -l app.kubernetes.io/name=containerd
kubectl get configmap -n containerd -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=containerd
```
### What is the problem you're trying to solve

Our K8s has a mixed deployment of Kata and Runc containers, where Kata containers runs untrusted workloads and Runc for the trusted workloads.

steps.title: Apply the fix for CRI support specify snapshotter during pod creation instead…
steps.description:
## What is this change
This is the implementation for https://github.com/containerd/containerd/issues/6657, the following lists core ideas:

1. Added `snapshotter` option in CRI per-runtime config
2. For containers related operations e.g. create containers, respect the runtime.snapshotter if set,
```yaml
[plugins."io.containerd.grpc.v1.cri".containerd.runtimes.my-runtime]
  snapshotter = "devmapper"
```

steps.title: Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n containerd -l app.kubernetes.io/name=containerd
kubectl get events -n containerd --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm the feature described in "CRI support specify snapshotter during pod creation instead…" is functioning correctly.

resolution.summary:
## What is this change
This is the implementation for https://github.com/containerd/containerd/issues/6657, the following lists core ideas:

1. Added `snapshotter` option in CRI per-runtime config
2. For containers related operations e.g. create containers, respect the runtime.snapshotter if set, otherwise using the global  snapshotter.

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with containerd installed or the issue environment reproducible.
